payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman because the system determined that mem_new is the newer/authoritative version of the same underlying fact. It filtered mem_old since that earlier memory was marked as superseded by mem_new for the same entity (Sam) in the Goldman context.
payload.decision: Selected mem_new for Sam from Goldman; filtered out mem_old because it is superseded.

entity_resolution.reason: Both memory items appear to refer to the same target entity (Sam) and the same contextual qualifier (Goldman), so the newer item is treated as the authoritative replacement.

payload.intent: Explain recall behavior for Sam (Goldman) where mem_new was returned and mem_old was filtered as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman.
Filtered mem_old because it is superseded by mem_new for the same entity and claim.

details: Applied supersession/conflict policy: newer replacement preferred for overlapping entity+claim.
Excluded older overlapping memory as superseded.

repair_options: If mem_old should not be replaced, ensure the supersession rule is not triggered (e.g., make mem_new non-overlapping or adjust overlap/priority criteria).
If mem_new is incorrect, mark mem_new as invalid or lower its priority so mem_old can surface.
Add explicit disambiguators (e.g., dates, roles, or qualifiers) so the system can determine whether the two memories truly refer to the same claim.
